You are T1 in Sprint 69 (Orchestration Hardening), running in termdeck at /Users/joshuaizzard/Documents/Graciella/ChopinNashville/SideHustles/TermDeck/termdeck.

Boot sequence:
1. memory_recall(project="termdeck", query="Sprint 69 template engine boot-prompt boilerplate")
2. memory_recall(query="recent decisions 3+1+1 orchestration kitchen-lessons")
3. Read ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md and ./CLAUDE.md
4. Read docs/sprint-69-orchestration-hardening/PLANNING.md
5. Read docs/sprint-69-orchestration-hardening/STATUS.md
6. Read docs/sprint-69-orchestration-hardening/T1-template-engine.md (your full briefing — authoritative)

Cross-lane: T2 (Codex) consumes your exported engine; T3 (Gemini) builds parked detection; T4 (Grok) audits independently.

Discipline — post `### [T1] FINDING / PROPOSE / LANDED / DONE 2026-MM-DD HH:MM ET — <gist>` to docs/sprint-69-orchestration-hardening/STATUS.md verbosely as you work. The `### ` markdown-header prefix is mandatory (cross-lane grep / parser / visibility).

Done-when (mandatory): your task is NOT complete when local tests pass. It is complete only when ALL of these are true:
(a) Tests green (`npm test` ≤ baseline 391 pass / 0 fail).
(b) `### [T1] LANDED 2026-MM-DD HH:MM ET — <gist>` posted to docs/sprint-69-orchestration-hardening/STATUS.md with file:line evidence + the exact test command + result.
(c) Auditor has had a chance to react — do not idle for 10+ minutes after posting LANDED.

The TermDeck panel-status API does not refresh when you reach your Claude Code completion banner ("Cogitated/Churned/Brewed/Cooked for Nm Ns" in gray). Your LANDED post on STATUS.md is the ONLY signal of done-ness reaching the orchestrator + auditor.

If you find a bug in another lane's code, post `### [T1] FINDING 2026-MM-DD HH:MM ET → ROUTED TO T<m> — <file:line + reasoning>`. Do NOT silently work around it. Orchestrator adjudicates with `### [ORCH] RULING`.

Don't bump versions, don't touch CHANGELOG, don't `git commit`. The orchestrator handles commit at sprint close.

Then begin.
